behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: Real failures are likely at the local handoff between merchant-category config resolution and score computation (e.g., wrong category key, stale/default threshold). A pure unit test of only the scoring function can miss those wiring errors.
behaviors.test_description: Run scorer through the real local scoring pipeline (config loader/resolver + scoring component) with a fixture config containing category-specific thresholds. Submit a transaction that exceeds only its category threshold and assert the produced numeric risk score matches the configured rule effect.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: This is a fraud-classification quality claim about the model itself. Mocking or stubbing the model would hide real misclassification behavior; the test must exercise the real model output behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real risk model with a labeled fraud-pattern fixture set where individual rule thresholds are not crossed, and assert fraud probability/classification exceeds the review threshold for known-positive cases (with tolerance over repeated runs if output is non-deterministic).

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Counting correctness often fails in module boundaries: repository query filters, timestamp normalization, and window-boundary inclusivity. Unit tests with mocked data access can miss these local wiring/state bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real local transaction repository path (test DB or filesystem fixture) plus scoring service. Seed per-user transactions around window boundaries, execute velocity scoring, and assert the count used in risk scoring matches configured inclusive/exclusive rules.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is orchestration logic across two model outputs and review-decision routing. A single-function boolean unit test may miss real wiring issues (field mapping, default/null handling, adapter translation).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real decisioning service with one model result set to flagged and the other set to not flagged through their normal local adapters, then assert transaction status is HOLD_FOR_REVIEW and audit fields indicate OR-based trigger.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: This is a local data-contract composition problem across scorer output, risk-model output, and response assembler. Mock-only unit tests can miss dropped/renamed fields during object mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end local composition (scoring result + risk result + response mapper) and assert output schema includes risk_score, fraud_probability, per-model flag indicators, and explanation/reason fields with correct source attribution.
